shape_decode: cmp=L, vp=W, vpc=W, naive=W -> cmplog BLOCKS; value_profile + value_profile_cmplog + naive RESOLVE. Mixed shape: each branch carries an i2s-ANTI claim (_h0: naive>cmplog, I2S hurts) AND a vp-PRO claim (_h1: vpc>cmplog, CMP_MAP gradient rescues). Per contract, each hypothesis is assigned by its named mechanism+direction, not by the shape label.

hypotheses.label: I2S RandReplace overwrites the exact structural/category/literal bytes naive havoc assembles, depleting (not enriching) the target byte in cmplog's corpus relative to naive

params.note: harfbuzz on-disk corpus available; these branches expose a fixed-ish offset structural/category byte (Halant H bytes, VPre record bytes, intact tag substrings, the 64/a8 repeated-record stride) that I2S substitution displaces

hypotheses.prediction: If I2S is ACTIVELY CORRUPTING the structural byte, cmplog's corpus is byte-DEPLETED at the target offset vs naive => signed_target_enrich < 0 (anti). An I2S-IRRELEVANT gate (the no-preimage class below) would instead return no_gate_signature; a pro-I2S gate would return signed_target_enrich > 0. So a clearly NEGATIVE enrichment discriminates active corruption from mere irrelevance.
hypotheses.g1_alternative_ruled_out: Rules out the 'I2S simply has no traction here' (no_gate_signature) and 'I2S helps' (positive enrich) alternatives: only ACTIVE depletion of the structural byte in the cmplog corpus produces signed_target_enrich < -0.3.
hypotheses.g2_independence: operand_enrichment compares cmplog-vs-naive corpus byte frequency, never the vp/vpc resolve signal that defined the shape; independent of the label-source.

hypotheses.label: Gate has no input-side CMP preimage (category-table / state-machine classification, length threshold, or internally-derived overflow sentinel); I2S substitution finds no fixed-offset value to copy, so it misallocates budget while naive havoc explores freely

params.note: operand_enrichment returns no_gate_signature on structural/length gates and on gates with no fixed-offset value operand; libpng_3974 has NO on-disk corpus (data_realities) so it is unscored and falls to fallback by absence-of-signal, not by a positive measurement

hypotheses.prediction: These gates have NO fixed-offset input-byte value to substitute (category-table dispatch, syllable-length threshold start+3<=end, derived overflow sentinel 0x7fffffff with no input preimage). operand_enrichment therefore returns no_gate_signature. A genuine fixed-offset value gate (the corruption class above) would instead return a definite signed_target_enrich; so 'no_gate_signature' positively discriminates the no-preimage class from byte-corruption.
hypotheses.g1_alternative_ruled_out: Distinguishes the no-input-preimage class (no fixed-offset operand => no_gate_signature) from the active-corruption class (a real target byte => a numeric signed enrich). If the tool DID find a target offset and return a number here, this hypothesis would be REFUTED for that branch.
hypotheses.g2_independence: Same corpus-byte-frequency measurement, independent of the vp/vpc resolve pattern.
hypotheses.collapse_note: MERGED three prior clusters (category_dispatch_misdirection, length_dimension_pinning, no_preimage_derived_sentinel). operand_enrichment cannot tell them apart -- all three present NO fixed-offset value operand and yield the identical no_gate_signature outcome. Per the collapse rule, candidates no real-data measurement can discriminate are merged. Their source-level distinction (table vs length vs sentinel) is real but UNMEASURABLE with built tools.

hypotheses.label: VP CMP_MAP Hamming/prefix-distance gradient gives graded partial-progress feedback toward the gate comparand, letting value_profile/vpc retain near-miss inputs that approach the target while cmplog's edge-only binary feedback stalls

proposed_tool.name: vp_gradient_climb
proposed_tool.measures: For the gate comparand, the minimum value-distance (Hamming / |operand - target|) reached by value_profile's corpus vs cmplog's corpus over the trial; alternatively depth_reach = how far into the comparison/category-state chain each fuzzer's best seed advances. Source: coverage_timeseries (snapshot corpora per checkpoint) or seed_set (final resolving/blocking seeds).
proposed_tool.build_note: Must measure value_profile's APPROACH (gradient effect), NOT a mutator event -- per data_realities, value_profile is a FEEDBACK invisible in mutation_op; its effect is only visible via what the corpus reaches (value-distance / depth), never via a lineage gradient event. Score harfbuzz from on-disk corpus; libpng has no corpus so vp_pro is unscorable for 3974 (falls to fallback).

params.note: comparand examples: 0x7fffffff (libpng redy sentinel), category/codepoint equality states (harfbuzz indic/myanmar/USE category gates, 0x0D12 MALAYALAM codepoint)

hypotheses.prediction: If VP's gradient is the rescue mechanism, value_profile's corpus REACHES strictly closer to the gate comparand than cmplog's (vp_min_value_distance < cmp_min_value_distance => distance_lift > 1) while cmplog plateaus far from the target. If instead both reached equal distance, the rescue would NOT be gradient-driven (refuted). This discriminates a true graded-approach rescue from a coincidental throughput/structural win.
hypotheses.g1_alternative_ruled_out: Rules out 'value_profile resolves for a non-gradient reason' (e.g. raw throughput like naive): under that alternative vp and cmplog would reach equal value-distance and distance_lift ~ 1. A gradient rescue REQUIRES vp to approach measurably closer than cmplog.
hypotheses.g2_independence: Measures corpus value-distance/depth to the comparand, NOT the vpc-resolves flag that defined the shape. The shape says vpc RESOLVES; this test asks WHETHER it got there by graded approach -- an independent question (G2 satisfied).

decision_order_rationale: Per branch, _h0 (i2s-anti) and _h1 (vp-pro) are INDEPENDENT claims about the same branch and BOTH may confirm (this shape mixes an i2s-anti and a vp-pro mechanism on every branch). The arbiter evaluates the i2s-anti axis first: try byte-corruption (definite negative enrich), else no-preimage (no_gate_signature). Then independently evaluate the vp-pro axis for _h1. A branch can be evidence-confirmed on one axis, both, or neither.
data_realities_respected: I2S is untagged in mutation_op: NO seed_lineage 'I2S event' descriptor proposed; operand_enrichment uses corpus BYTE FREQUENCY (depletion), not a lineage event.
value_profile is a FEEDBACK not a mutator: vp_pro descriptor proposed over coverage_timeseries/seed_set value-distance/depth_reach (what the corpus REACHES), NOT a lineage gradient event.

On-disk corpora exist only for curl/harfbuzz/openthread/sqlite3: 10/11 branches are harfbuzz (scorable); libpng_3974 has NO corpus -> both its _h0 (operand_enrichment) and _h1 (vp_gradient_climb) are UNSCORABLE and fall to fallback=inconclusive by absence of signal.

Structural-assembly gates have different-sized W/L seeds so byte-alignment can fail: the no-preimage class explicitly EXPECTS no_gate_signature rather than forcing a byte_freq comparison; only the corruption class (fixed-ish offset category/tag/record bytes) uses byte_freq.

notes.merges: Collapsed 5 prior clusters into 3 hypotheses across 2 axes. i2s-anti axis: (a) i2s_cmp_target_corrupts_structural_bytes -> i2s_anti_structural_byte_corruption (kept separate: measurable byte depletion); (b) i2s_category_dispatch_misdirection + i2s_length_dimension_pinning + i2s_no_preimage_derived_sentinel -> i2s_anti_no_input_preimage_misdirection (MERGED: all yield no_gate_signature, indiscriminable). vp-pro axis: vp_cmpmap_gradient_rescues_cmplog_binary_stall -> vp_pro_cmpmap_gradient_rescue (kept; 11 members, all same gradient mechanism).
notes.targets: 10/11 branches harfbuzz (indic/myanmar/USE shapers + vowel-constraints), 1 libpng (cHRM redy sentinel). Only harfbuzz is corpus-scorable.
notes.tools_not_built: vp_pro_cmpmap_gradient_rescue requires a NEW tool 'vp_gradient_climb' (registry_tool: null) measuring value_distance_reached / depth_reach to the comparand. NOT YET BUILT -- orchestrator must build+register from the proposed descriptor before this hypothesis can be arbitrated. The two i2s-anti hypotheses REUSE the existing operand_enrichment tool.
notes.discriminability_caveat: The i2s-anti axis leans on the corruption-vs-no_preimage split being CLEAN in operand_enrichment output. If a no-preimage branch happens to expose a spurious fixed-offset byte and returns a numeric enrich near 0 (not < -0.3), it correctly fails the corruption rule and is NOT mislabeled; it then matches neither i2s rule cleanly and falls to fallback for the i2s axis. Honest 'no clean i2s test' is preferred over forcing a label.
